BoostedIVForest: Boosted causal forest with instrumental variables

Source: R/causalBoosted_IV.R
BoostedIVForest.Rd

BoostedIVForest: Boosted causal forest with instrumental variables

BoostedIVForest: Boosted causal forest with instrumental variables

Value

Object returned by BoostedIVForest.

Details

Combines CXGBoost cross-fitted nuisance estimation with grf::instrumental_forest to estimate heterogeneous Local Average Treatment Effects (LATE) in the presence of endogenous treatment.

Estimation pipeline

  1. Impute missing values in X using per-column medians.

  2. Cross-fit nuisances (parallelised, n_folds folds):

    • \(\hat m(X) = E[Y \mid X]\)

    • \(\hat e(X) = E[W \mid X]\)

    • \(\hat p(X) = E[Z \mid X]\)

  3. Residualise: \(\tilde Y = Y - \hat m(X)\), \(\tilde W = W - \hat e(X)\), \(\tilde Z = Z - \hat p(X)\).

  4. Fit grf::instrumental_forest on \((\tilde Y, \tilde W, \tilde Z, X)\).

Parallelism

Cross-fitting is parallelised via future / future.apply. Set a parallel plan before calling $fit():


  future::plan(future::multisession, workers = 4)
  model$fit(X, Y, W, Z)
  future::plan(future::sequential)   # reset afterwards

See also

RCausalML-package

Public fields

n_folds

Number of cross-fitting folds.

nrounds

XGBoost boosting rounds per fold.

xgb_params

Extra XGBoost hyperparameters.

forest_args

Extra arguments for grf::instrumental_forest.

forest

Fitted grf::instrumental_forest (post-fit).

Y_hat

Out-of-fold \(E[Y|X]\) (post-fit).

W_hat

Out-of-fold \(E[W|X]\) (post-fit).

Z_hat

Out-of-fold \(E[Z|X]\) (post-fit).

iv_diagnostics

Weak-instrument diagnostics list (post-fit).

imputation_table

Per-column medians used for NA imputation (post-fit).

train_colnames

Column names of the training X (post-fit).

Methods

Public methods

  • BoostedIVForest$new()

  • BoostedIVForest$fit()

  • BoostedIVForest$predict()

  • BoostedIVForest$average_treatment_effect()

  • BoostedIVForest$test_calibration()

  • BoostedIVForest$variable_importance()

  • BoostedIVForest$summary()

  • BoostedIVForest$evaluate()

  • BoostedIVForest$subgroup_analysis()

  • BoostedIVForest$plot_cate()

  • BoostedIVForest$save_model()

  • BoostedIVForest$clone()

Method new()

Create a new BoostedIVForest.

Usage

BoostedIVForest$new(
  n_folds = 5L,
  nrounds = 100L,
  xgb_params = list(),
  forest_args = list()
)

Arguments

n_folds

Number of cross-fitting folds (integer >= 2, default 5).

nrounds

XGBoost boosting rounds per fold (integer >= 1, default 100).

xgb_params

Named list of extra XGBoost hyperparameters passed to CXGBoost$new(parameters = ...).

forest_args

Named list of extra arguments for grf::instrumental_forest() (e.g. num.trees, min.node.size, honesty.fraction).

Method fit()

Fit the boosted IV forest.

Usage

BoostedIVForest$fit(X, Y, W, Z, verbose = TRUE)

Arguments

X

Numeric covariate matrix (n x p). NAs are median-imputed.

Y

Observed outcome vector (length n).

W

Endogenous treatment, binary or continuous (length n).

Z

Instrument, binary or continuous (length n). Must satisfy relevance, exclusion restriction, and exogeneity.

verbose

Logical; print stage progress (default TRUE).

Returns

self invisibly (supports method chaining).

Method predict()

Predict heterogeneous LATE for new units.

Usage

BoostedIVForest$predict(X_new = NULL, estimate.variance = FALSE, ...)

Arguments

X_new

Numeric covariate matrix. If NULL, returns in-sample OOB predictions. Column order must match training X; NAs are imputed.

estimate.variance

Logical; return variance estimates (default FALSE).

...

Extra arguments forwarded to predict.instrumental_forest.

Returns

A grf prediction list with element predictions (and optionally variance.estimates).

Method average_treatment_effect()

Estimate the ATE with standard error.

Usage

BoostedIVForest$average_treatment_effect()

Returns

Named numeric vector: estimate and std.err.

Method test_calibration()

Best linear predictor calibration test for heterogeneity.

A significant differential.forest.prediction coefficient indicates genuine heterogeneity captured by the model. Not supported for instrumental_forest; returns NULL in that case.

Usage

BoostedIVForest$test_calibration()

Returns

lm object from grf::test_calibration, or NULL.

Method variable_importance()

Variable importance (proportion of splits per covariate).

Usage

BoostedIVForest$variable_importance()

Returns

Named numeric vector, sorted descending.

Method summary()

Print a formatted model summary.

Usage

BoostedIVForest$summary(top_n = 5L)

Arguments

top_n

Number of top variables to display (default 5).

Returns

self invisibly.

Method evaluate()

Evaluate CATE estimates against true treatment effects.

Requires ground-truth tau_true (available in simulations).

Usage

BoostedIVForest$evaluate(tau_true, X_new = NULL)

Arguments

tau_true

Numeric vector of true individual treatment effects.

X_new

Optional covariate matrix for out-of-sample evaluation. If NULL, uses in-sample OOB predictions.

Returns

Named list: PEHE, ATE_error, tau_hat.

Method subgroup_analysis()

Split units into high / low CATE subgroups and compute group-level ATEs via grf::average_treatment_effect.

Usage

BoostedIVForest$subgroup_analysis(X_new = NULL, quantile = 0.5)

Arguments

X_new

Optional covariate matrix (uses training data if NULL).

quantile

Threshold quantile for "high" group (default 0.5 = median split).

Returns

A named list with components:

tau_hat

Full CATE vector.

high_idx

Row indices of the high-CATE group.

low_idx

Row indices of the low-CATE group.

ATE_high

ATE estimate in the high-CATE group.

ATE_low

ATE estimate in the low-CATE group.

Method plot_cate()

Plot CATE distribution and top-variable partial dependence.

Requires ggplot2. Produces two panels:

  1. Density of \(\hat\tau(x)\) with a vertical ATE line.

  2. Scatter of the top covariate vs \(\hat\tau(x)\).

Usage

BoostedIVForest$plot_cate(X_new = NULL, top_var = NULL, ...)

Arguments

X_new

Optional covariate matrix. Uses training data if NULL.

top_var

Name or column index of the covariate for the scatter panel. If NULL, the highest-importance variable is used.

...

Extra arguments forwarded to ggplot2::geom_density.

Returns

A ggplot object (invisibly).

Method save_model()

Save the fitted model to disk.

Usage

BoostedIVForest$save_model(path)

Arguments

path

File path (e.g. "model.rds").

Returns

self invisibly.

Method clone()

The objects of this class are cloneable with this method.

Usage

BoostedIVForest$clone(deep = FALSE)

Arguments

deep

Whether to make a deep clone.

Examples

if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{
# Basic usage
# BoostedIVForest(...)
} # }
